Title: Structure and evolution of alanine/serine decarboxylases and the engineering of theanine production

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: important indicator of green tea quality; health effects and medicinal value; applications in food and cosmetics
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glutamine synthetase used here as theanine synthetase surrogate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes L-theanine synthesis from ethylamine and glutamate in vitro when combined with CsAlaDC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro two-enzyme synthesis assay; UPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsGS functioned as a theanine synthetase in the reconstructed in vitro pathway. 2. Combining PsGS with engineered CsAlaDC variants increased theanine production over wild-type CsAlaDC combinations. 3. PsGS supports biocatalytic theanine production from alanine-derived ethylamine and glutamate.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful partner enzyme for synthetic/industrial theanine biosynthesis modules, especially together with improved CsAlaDC alleles
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: heterologous microbial enzyme used for pathway reconstruction rather than a native tea core gene
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro reaction containing Ala, Glu, ATP, MgCl2, PLP, HEPES pH 7.5

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: important indicator of green tea quality; health effects and medicinal value; applications in food and cosmetics
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: gamma-glutamylmethylamide synthetase/gamma-glutamate methylamine ligase used as theanine synthetase surrogate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes L-theanine synthesis from ethylamine and glutamate in vitro when combined with CsAlaDC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro two-enzyme synthesis assay; UPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MmGMAS functioned as a theanine synthetase in the reconstructed in vitro pathway. 2. Pairing MmGMAS with engineered CsAlaDC, especially L110F/P114A, gave the highest theanine output. 3. MmGMAS is suitable for industrially oriented theanine biocatalysis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate enzyme for microbial/cell-free theanine production platforms with improved CsAlaDC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: heterologous microbial enzyme used for pathway reconstruction rather than a native tea core gene
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro reaction containing Ala, Glu, ATP, MgCl2, PLP, HEPES pH 7.5

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Tea plant; Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: important indicator of green tea quality; health effects and medicinal value; applications in food, cosmetics, and medicine
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ethylamine supply step of theanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase with distinctive C-terminal zinc finger
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: decarboxylates alanine to produce ethylamine, the precursor for theanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for ethylamine supply; wild-type enzyme has lower catalytic activity than SerDC homologs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transient overexpression in N. benthamiana leaves led to ethylamine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ethylamine formation from alanine and thereby supports L-theanine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; crystal structures; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro coupled theanine synthesis; UPLC and GC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsAlaDC specifically catalyzes alanine decarboxylation to ethylamine, unlike SerDCs that use serine. 2. CsAlaDC has substantially lower catalytic capacity than AtSerDC/CsSerDC, consistent with evolutionary reduction of activity. 3. Phe106 determines alanine substrate specificity, Tyr336 is essential for catalysis, and L110F/P114A strongly enhances activity and boosts theanine production in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: prime target for gene editing or enzyme engineering to raise ethylamine and theanine production in tea or heterologous production systems; stacking with theanine synthetase is recommended
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: originated from CsSerDC by gene duplication and neofunctionalization; truncated zinc-finger-deleted protein became insoluble, indicating zinc finger contributes to stability
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: enzyme assays at pH 8.0 and 45°C with PLP; transient assays in 5-week-old N. benthamiana; in vitro theanine synthesis with Ala, Glu, ATP, Mg2+
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: structural biology datasets in RCSB PDB: 8JIK (alanine decarboxylase), 8JIJ (alanine decarboxylase complex)
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsAlaDC monomer partner in homodimer; PsGS; MmGMAS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: authors hypothesize excessive ethylamine could be toxic/harmful to plants if overaccumulated; raising activity may require caution
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA1305 constitutive transient expression vector in Nicotiana benthamiana

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: important indicator of green tea quality; health effects and medicinal value; applications in food and cosmetics
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ethylamine supply step of theanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase engineered at dimer interface
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: engineered CsAlaDC variant with enhanced decarboxylation of alanine to ethylamine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: engineered higher-activity version of the key ethylamine-supply enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: substantially increases L-theanine output in coupled in vitro synthesis relative to wild-type CsAlaDC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay; in vitro coupled theanine synthesis; UPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The L110F/P114A double mutant raised CsAlaDC catalytic activity about 2.3-fold over wild type. 2. When coupled with PsGS or MmGMAS, this variant markedly increased theanine production. 3. Dimer-interface engineering is an effective route to optimize industrial theanine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for metabolic engineering, industrial cell-free synthesis, or microbial production of theanine; can guide genome editing of native CsAlaDC
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: mutations lie at the dimerization interface and likely increase hydrophobicity to enhance activity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro reaction containing Ala, Glu, ATP, MgCl2, PLP, HEPES pH 7.5
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: potential risk of excessive ethylamine accumulation if deployed in planta was hypothesized

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: product of serine decarboxylation in plant metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase with distinctive C-terminal zinc finger
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transient overexpression in N. benthamiana leaves did not produce detectable ethylamine unless Y111F mutated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: specifically produces ethanolamine from serine and lacks alanine decarboxylase activity in wild type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; crystal structure; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; UPLC/GC-MS product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSerDC specifically catalyzes serine decarboxylation and has higher catalytic efficiency than CsAlaDC. 2. Tyr111 is a key substrate-specificity residue favoring serine, whereas Tyr341 is essential for catalytic activity. 3. Mutation Y111F confers alanine decarboxylase activity and ethylamine production in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: template enzyme for understanding/evolving substrate specificity and for engineering new alanine decarboxylases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: used as representative SerDC for structural comparison because AtSerDC and CsSerDC had similar catalytic properties
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: enzyme assays at pH 8.0 and 40°C with PLP; transient assays in 5-week-old N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: structural biology dataset in RCSB PDB: 8JG7
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA1305 constitutive transient expression vector in Nicotiana benthamiana

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: product of serine decarboxylation in plant metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: specifically catalyzes serine decarboxylation and has higher catalytic efficiency than CsAlaDC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; UPLC product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsSerDC catalyzes serine but not alanine decarboxylation. 2. CsSerDC has catalytic efficiency similar to AtSerDC and much higher than CsAlaDC. 3. CsSerDC supports the conclusion that CsAlaDC evolved from SerDC with altered substrate specificity and reduced activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: reference enzyme for comparative engineering of CsAlaDC activity and specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: used as tea SerDC comparator; not structurally characterized in this study
